heading: Graduate and undergraduate language and area centers and programs
label: 1122 Graduate and undergraduate language and area centers and programs

Amendments2008—Subsec. (a)(1)(A). , added subpar. (A) and struck out former subpar. (A). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Secretary is authorized—Pub. L. 110–315, § 602(1)(A)“(i) to make grants to institutions of higher education, or combinations thereof, for the purpose of establishing, strengthening, and operating comprehensive foreign language and area or international studies centers and programs; and“(ii) to make grants to such institutions or combinations for the purpose of establishing, strengthening, and operating a diverse network of undergraduate foreign language and area or international studies centers and programs.”Subsec. (a)(2)(I), (J). , added subpars. (I) and (J).Pub. L. 110–315, § 602(1)(B)Subsec. (a)(4)(C). , added subpar. (C). Former subpar. (C) redesignated (D).Pub. L. 110–315, § 602(1)(C)(iv), substituted “Partnerships or programs of linkage and outreach” for “Programs of linkage or outreach” and inserted “, including Federal or State scholarship programs for students in related areas” before period at end.Pub. L. 110–315, § 602(1)(C)(i)Subsec. (a)(4)(D). , redesignated subpar. (C) as (D). Former subpar. (D) redesignated (E).Pub. L. 110–315, § 602(1)(C)(iii)Subsec. (a)(4)(E). , redesignated subpar. (D) as (E). Former subpar. (E) redesignated (F).Pub. L. 110–315, § 602(1)(C)(iii), substituted “area studies” for “foreign area” and “(D), and (E)” for “(C), and (D)” and struck out “of linkage and outreach” after “programs”.Pub. L. 110–315, § 602(1)(C)(ii)Subsec. (a)(4)(F). , redesignated subpar. (E) as (F).Pub. L. 110–315, § 602(1)(C)(iii)Subsec. (b). , struck out “Graduate” before “fellowships” in heading.Pub. L. 110–315, § 602(2)(A)Subsec. (b)(2). , added par. (2) and struck out former par. (2). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Students receiving stipends described in paragraph (1) shall be individuals who are engaged in an instructional program with stated performance goals for functional foreign language use or in a program developing such performance goals, in combination with area studies, international studies, or the international aspects of a professional studies program, including predissertation level studies, preparation for dissertation research, dissertation research abroad, and dissertation writing.”Pub. L. 110–315, § 602(2)(B)Subsecs. (d), (e). , added subsecs. (d) and (e) and struck out former subsec. (d). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Stipends awarded to graduate level recipients may include allowances for dependents and for travel for research and study in the United States and abroad.”Pub. L. 110–315, § 602(3)

children.heading: National language and area centers and programs authorized
children.label: a National language and area centers and programs authorized
i
children.text: comprehensive foreign language and area or international studies centers and programs; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a diverse network of undergraduate foreign language and area or international studies centers and programs.

children.label: i
children.text: teaching of any modern foreign language;

children.label: ii
children.text: instruction in fields needed to provide full understanding of areas, regions, or countries in which such language is commonly used;

children.label: iii
children.text: research and training in international studies, and the international and foreign language aspects of professional and other fields of study; and

children.label: iv
children.text: instruction and research on issues in world affairs that concern one or more countries.

children.label: A
children.text: teaching and research materials;

children.label: B
children.text: curriculum planning and development;

children.label: C
children.text: establishing and maintaining linkages with overseas institutions of higher education and other organizations that may contribute to the teaching and research of the center or program;

children.label: D
children.text: bringing visiting scholars and faculty to the center to teach or to conduct research;

children.label: E
children.text: professional development of the center’s faculty and staff;

children.label: F
children.text: projects conducted in cooperation with other centers addressing themes of world regional, cross-regional, international, or global importance;

children.label: G
children.text: summer institutes in the United States or abroad designed to provide language and area training in the center’s field or topic;

children.label: H
children.text: support for faculty, staff, and student travel in foreign areas, regions, or countries, and for the development and support of educational programs abroad for students;

children.label: I
children.text: supporting instructors of the less commonly taught languages; and

children.label: J
children.text: projects that support students in the science, technology, engineering, and mathematics fields to achieve foreign language proficiency.

children.heading: Grants to maintain library collections
children.label: 3 Grants to maintain library collections
children.text: The Secretary may make grants to centers described in paragraph (1) having important library collections, as determined by the Secretary, for the maintenance of such collections.

children.label: A
children.text: Programs of linkage or outreach between foreign language, area studies, or other international fields, and professional schools and colleges.

children.label: B
children.text: Programs of linkage or outreach with 2- and 4-year colleges and universities.

children.label: i
children.text: postsecondary programs or departments in foreign language, area studies, or other international fields; and

children.label: ii
children.text: State educational agencies or local educational agencies.

children.label: D
children.text: Partnerships or programs of linkage and outreach with departments or agencies of Federal and State governments, including Federal or State scholarship programs for students in related areas.

children.label: E
children.text: Programs of linkage or outreach with the news media, business, professional, or trade associations.

children.label: F
children.text: Summer institutes in area studies, foreign language, and other international fields designed to carry out the programs described in subparagraphs (A), (B), (D), and (E).

children.heading: Fellowships for foreign language and area or international studies
children.label: b Fellowships for foreign language and area or international studies

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to make grants to institutions of higher education or combinations of such institutions for the purpose of paying stipends to individuals undergoing advanced training in any center or program approved by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: in an instructional program with stated performance goals for functional foreign language use or in a program developing such performance goals, in combination with area studies, international studies, or the international aspects of a professional studies program; and

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of an undergraduate student, in the intermediate or advanced study of a less commonly taught language; or

children.label: I
children.text: predissertation level study;

children.label: II
children.text: preparation for dissertation research;

children.label: III
children.text: dissertation research abroad; or

children.label: IV
children.text: dissertation writing.

children.heading: Special rule with respect to travel
children.label: c Special rule with respect to travel
children.text: No funds may be expended under this part for undergraduate travel except in accordance with rules prescribed by the Secretary setting forth policies and procedures to assure that Federal funds made available for such travel are expended as part of a formal program of supervised study.

children.heading: Graduate level recipients
children.label: 1 Graduate level recipients
children.text: A stipend awarded to a graduate level recipient may include allowances for dependents and for travel for research and study in the United States and abroad.

children.label: A
children.text: are closely linked to the overall goals of the recipient’s course of study; and

children.label: B
children.text: have the purpose of promoting foreign language fluency and knowledge of foreign cultures.
an explanation of how the activities funded by the grant will reflect diverse perspectives and a wide range of views and generate debate on world regions and international affairs; and

a description of how the applicant will encourage government service in areas of national need, as identified by the Secretary, as well as in areas of need in the education, business, and nonprofit sectors.
